title: Compliance frameworks, DRS load balancing & OIDC role fix

items.text: Security compliance frameworks (Enterprise): assess a connection against NIST 800-53, NIST 800-171, CMMC Level 2 and ISO/IEC 27001 from a new Frameworks tab, with a score donut, satisfied / partial / failed breakdown, per-node results and a styled PDF report
Open in Proxmox: a button next to the cluster or node name opens the native Proxmox web interface in a new tab, deep-linking a member node to its own management IP
The migration target-network selector now lists SDN VNets alongside classic bridges, so nodes whose guest networks are VNets no longer get an empty list
Inventory Network view shows host bridges and VLANs per node, so clusters with no VMs are no longer empty; bridges open a detail panel and SDN VNet ids resolve to their friendly alias
Node maintenance is simpler: entering maintenance triggers Proxmox node-maintenance (HA guests evacuated by Proxmox) with a note that non-HA guests must be migrated or shut down manually

DRS now honors the Balance Types setting (previously a dead knob): load-balancing candidates are filtered by guest type for both reactive balancing and homogenization, with a guest-type gate applied at execution time
DRS settings cleanup: removed the dead balancing-method and balancing-mode knobs, guarded balance-types at a minimum of one, one-row resource weights, and status icons on exclusions and balance-types
The inventory tree expand/collapse state is now scoped per tenant, so switching tenant no longer carries over the previous expansion
The cluster SSH test shows the per-node ok / error breakdown on failure too, not just on success
The VM delete confirmation dialog spells out exactly what will be removed

OIDC role preserved on login: v1.4.4 demoted OIDC users with no IdP group match to viewer on every sign-in (locking out manually assigned admins); the re-sync is now authoritative only when a group-to-role mapping is configured and groups are sent
OIDC accounts are labelled correctly and can no longer be given a local password, closing a credentials login path that would bypass SSO and MFA
Warm and direct-ESXi migrations preserve the source NIC MAC, so the migrated guest keeps its network identity instead of stranding its IP on a ghost adapter
Warm migration reliability: a stale /dev/nbdN device is released before attaching and the delta-apply is chunked under the SSH argument-size limit
Migrating a running LXC container now uses restart mode instead of online=1 (which Proxmox rejects for containers), so an online CT migration no longer fails outright
Dropped the misleading "vSAN datastore detected" log line on the vCenter migration path
The percent chart Y-axis is widened so the "100%" label is no longer clipped
Old completed tasks are no longer re-alerted as new events about a week after they ran
Security: patched Go orchestrator CVEs by bumping golang.org/x/crypto, x/net and x/sys (SSH, HTML and IDNA advisories)
Header tenant switcher with per-tenant dashboards for MSP: switch the active tenant from the navbar and see dashboards scoped to that tenant
Shared migration tasks in the footer, visible across users so the whole team sees in-flight migrations, with a warm-setup docs link
VM console screenshots are served as JPEG instead of raw PPM, for lighter and faster previews
RRD performance charts show dates on the axes for multi-day timeframes, not just times
The Backups tab surfaces the real gateway error instead of failing with "Unexpected token '<'" when a proxy returns an HTML error page
A failed migration block-device transfer is reported as a failure instead of being masked as success
Warm migration to a thick target bounds the zero-fill so it can no longer exit on a false ENOSPC
Guest and node API routes no longer swallow real errors, so failures surface instead of empty data
OIDC users re-sync their role from IdP group membership on every login, so group changes take effect immediately
RRD performance graphs are scoped to the resource rather than the whole connection, closing an RBAC scope leak
The inventory NETWORK section stays stable when a connection briefly blips instead of collapsing
Replication writes the DR config with cp -f on pmxcfs, avoiding a transient failure window
Security: removed the unused npm from the runtime image, patched dependency CVEs (undici, OpenSSL on the orchestrator) and refreshed front-end dependencies

title: MSP mode, multi-license stacking & connection diagnostics

items.text: MSP mode: an MSP tenant can own entire Proxmox clusters (full-cluster view) alongside vDC / IaaS slicing; the provider / NOC sees the whole fleet for supervision and license aggregation
Provider provisioning of connections to MSP tenants from Settings, with a Tenant / vDC ownership column and an owner selector when creating a connection
MSP tenants get fleet-wide scope on their owned clusters: inventory, dashboard, alerts, reports, backup jobs and VM migration among owned connections

Multi-license stacking: import additional licenses to grow fleet capacity without regenerating the primary license, with fleet-total quota, per-tenant rollup, Licensed-to per import, edit-mapping and remove
Connection health diagnostics: a per-connection column and modal running read-only reachability, auth, version, cluster / Ceph, storage and SSH checks; works in Community mode
Guest names in VM and CT alerts and event emails: the guest name shows next to the vmid in the alerts table, the navbar dropdown and the email template
Connections Nodes column collapses to the first node plus a count, with the full list on hover
Deploy wizard exposes real bridges and an editable VLAN tag for provider and MSP modes
Green Score insight moves to its own row so longer suggestions stay readable
The What's New panel no longer opens automatically on a new version; open it any time from the profile menu

Orchestrator API authentication now reads PROXCENTER_API_API_KEY (the value docker-compose injects), so auth can be enabled and matches the frontend; an unset key keeps auth disabled as a safe fallback

title: Warm migration, SPICE consoles, Ceph topology & security

items.text: Warm migration for VMware with changed-block tracking and a final delta sync: no data loss on large or busy disks, for ESXi-direct and vCenter (including vSAN), single and bulk, with a go/no-go preflight
In-browser SPICE console for QEMU VMs, alongside noVNC
Ceph CRUSH topology view in the cluster Ceph tab: read-only CRUSH tree with details and pools
Clone a VM from a snapshot restore point, choosing a snapshot as the clone source
Role-level default RBAC scope, inherited by every assignment of that role
SSO-only login policy for OIDC (hide the local form, force the SSO redirect)
Local TOTP two-factor authentication with an admin enforcement policy
Local migration from the cluster Guests tab (node-to-node), not just cross-cluster
Reports and notifications: connection scoping, backup report polish, per-category severity, and an event-email rework (English copy, task-log details, one mail per event)
Ceph tab shows the full cluster config with working OSD flag toggles
Dashboard widgets honor the appearance settings: font-size, corner-rounding and a shared gauge
Guest VLANs resolved from host bond sub-interfaces so tagged guests group correctly
Backups show the real local backup time with Proxmox-style columns; the empty Backups tab explains why
Security hardening: TOFU host-key verification, per-connection ws-proxy TLS, dependency advisories cleared, Node 26 pipeline hardening
Partial-VM cleanup no longer leaks the target VMID after a failed conversion
Grant Sys.Modify in token setup, with an actionable Rolling Update permission warning
Backups 'Run now' works again (a missing route returned an HTML 404)
VM User role gains the read access the Inventory needs to load
Standalone hosts behind NAT: node management connects to the public host, not the private interface
Serial and headless VMs show a badge instead of looping on a failing screenshot
Resume paused VMs; allow dots in tags; the guest icon dims when off; tree sections stay open on header click

title: MSP / IaaS — vDC, IPAM, PBS multi-tenant & Postgres

items.text: vDC tenant cockpit (My vDC) with live consumption: hosts, VMs metrics, donut quotas (CPU, RAM, Storage, Snapshots, Backups), datacenter map and green KPIs
Cloud-style tenant deploy flow with quota enforcement on every step (template, ISO, clone, restore)
Native IPAM at the vDC level: SDN VNet/subnet management, automatic IP/MAC reservation on deploy, clone, qmrestore and config edit, plus PVE pool scan to merge externally created VMs
Per-vDC PBS bindings with auto-provisioning of namespace, sub-token, ACL and PVE storage; manual mode also supported
Tenant restore from PBS: overwrite source VM or restore as a new VM into the tenant vDC pool, with simple-mode dialog
PVE backup jobs scoped to tenant pools, with structured schedule picker (frequency, time, weekdays) and Verify / Delete actions
Datacenters, country and Green IT configuration: per-datacenter assignments tree, country flags on tenants and clusters, green factors per node
Tenant-scoped alerts, tasks, events and reports on shared-node clusters, with vDC report type gated to super-admin
Per-resource Failback toggle in the HA tab and refreshed HA UI
LXC network edit with IP, gateway and IPv6 fields; templates listed from any node hosting the storage
Cross-cluster offline migration shows real progress; choose target VMID for external migrations; VLAN tag applied on migrated VM NIC
WeasyPrint sidecar for scheduled report PDF rendering
Tenants 'Disabled' state renamed to 'Locked' with explicit semantics
RBAC grants are preloaded once per request, removing the N+1 in scoping helpers
Settings tabs are now scrollable on narrow screens; enterprise-only tabs are hidden in Community instead of greyed out
Alerts visibility for tenants is awaited consistently across every callsite (regression test added)
Tags persistence drops raw SQL placeholders and restores tenant scoping
vDC tenants can SSH on provider connections; restored VMs land in the tenant pool
Shared storages are no longer hidden from the tenant scope
VxlanTag is now allocated against the live PVE state (not just the local DB)
PBS timestamps drop milliseconds before being sent to PVE; backupId is parsed correctly when the namespace contains slashes
Network inventory: IP usage column, click-to-detail modal, no flicker
Cross-tenant isolation hardened on inventory, PBS backups and PVE backup jobs (tenant with zero vDCs is no longer treated as provider)

Database backend is now Postgres only. SQLite is no longer shipped or supported. Re-run the install script to upgrade (e.g. curl -fsSL https://proxcenter.io/install/community | sudo bash): it adds the Postgres service, generates POSTGRES_PASSWORD and the postgres_data volume. The frontend re-bootstraps its configuration via the UI on first boot.
Hyper-V migration — connect to Hyper-V hosts, browse VMs, and migrate to Proxmox via virt-v2v
Nutanix AHV migration — connect to Prism Central, download disks, and migrate to Proxmox via virt-v2v
vCenter migration — migrate VMs from VMware vCenter (in addition to direct ESXi)
Migration Dashboard — unified view with KPIs, recent jobs, and hypervisor logos
SSHFS Boot migration mode — near-zero downtime VM migration from ESXi
Dashboard overhaul — dark vignette widgets, new widget types, global time range picker
Multi-dashboard support — create multiple dashboards with tabs and drag-drop reorder
Auto-HA — automatically configure High Availability for VMs on clusters
Live VM screenshot preview — see a live console preview for QEMU VMs
Datacenter Settings tab — configure PVE tag styles, general options, and more
LXC template listing — browse available CT templates in the creation wizard
Markdown notes — render Markdown in VM, node, and cluster descriptions
Hardware System section — BIOS, Machine Type, Display, SCSI Controller management
Configurable energy currency for Green IT cost display
Rolling update reboot prediction and skip indicator in wizard
Tags now display with PVE colors, shapes (full/dense/circle), and responsive overflow in all tables
DRS recommendations grouped by cluster with balance graph in drawer
Expandable charts everywhere — CPU, RAM, Ceph, Storage, with proper axes
VM summary panel — vCPU count, disk list, storage usage bar via guest agent
HA inline selector, console OS overlay, and chart color improvements
Cluster view — HA & Ceph UI improvements, node trends, affinity rules
Backup job dialog redesigned with HA resource management
Taskbar always dark in light mode with Proxmox node icons
LXC containers — swap editing and disk display
Migration pipeline — virtio-scsi driver, NTFS dirty flag recovery, guest tools firstboot injection
Top Consumers widget now persists CPU/Memory tab selection across page refreshes
DRS notification bell now matches the Recommendations tab (validates stale entries)
VM migration dialog now correctly detects local vs shared storage from PVE API
Tag colors consistent with Proxmox datacenter.cfg color-map across all views
Security: updated vite 8.0.2 to 8.0.5 (3 CVEs patched)
Real-time network traffic visualization powered by sFlow and Open vSwitch
Sankey flow diagram — interactive Source → Application → Destination visualization with animated flows
Time series bandwidth charts per VM with InfluxDB storage
Top Talkers, Top Pairs, and Top Ports overview with click-through details
sFlow agent management — configure OVS sFlow directly from the UI
Configurable sampling rate with recommendations based on network speed
InfluxDB integration for historical time series data
Full German (Deutsch) translation — available in the language selector
Multi-tenant support — isolate and manage multiple environments from a single ProxCenter instance
Tenant management panel in Settings (super admin only)
Per-tenant data isolation — connections, VMs, and configurations scoped to each tenant
Seamless tenant switching from the dashboard
Bulk migration — migrate multiple VMs at once from ESXi or XCP-ng to Proxmox
Live migration support — migrate running VMs with minimal downtime
ESXi migration pipeline with disk download, conversion (qemu-img) and import
XCP-ng migration pipeline with XVA export and automated conversion
Real-time progress tracking per VM during bulk migrations
